items.original: Меня опять терзают смутные сомнения… У Шпака — магнитофон, у посла — медальон
items.translation:
Vague doubts haunt me once again... Shpak's tape recorder, the ambassador's medallion.
Shpak's tape recorder,
the ambassador's medallion.
items.reference: Vague doubts haunt me once again... Shpak's tape recorder, the ambassador's medallion.
items.analysis: Core line matches the reference exactly and preserves the mock-heroic register of «терзают смутные сомнения». It also correctly avoids turning «у Шпака / у посла» into simple possession in the present moment (the reference notes the loss/association reading). The only problem is the immediate repetition across two subtitle entries: the second two-line repeat is not in the reference item and reads like an accidental duplicate, which can blunt the punch and pacing.

items.original: Здравствуйте, царь, очень приятно. Царь, очень приятно, царь. Очень приятно, здравствуйте, царь.
items.translation:
Hello, I’m the Tsar, very pleased to meet you. The Tsar, very pleased. Tsar, very pleased. Hello, the Tsar.

The Tsar, very pleased. Hello, the Tsar. Very pleased!
items.reference: Hello, I’m the Tsar, very pleased to meet you. The Tsar, very pleased. Tsar, very pleased. Hello, the Tsar.
items.analysis: The first subtitle block is identical to the reference and perfectly captures the joke: repetitive, mundane Soviet politeness colliding with royal context. However, the translation then adds an extra repeated block (“The Tsar, very pleased…” etc.) that is not part of the reference item. Even if the film audio continues, relative to this specific creative passage the added repetition risks overplaying the gag and slightly distorting the calibrated awkwardness.

items.translation: I demand that the banquet continue!
items.reference: I demand that the banquet continue!
items.analysis: Identical to the reference; preserves the sudden authoritarian, power-hungry performative tone that makes the line funny in context.

items.original: Оставь меня, старушка, я в печали
items.translation: Leave me, old woman, I am in sorrow.
items.reference: Leave me, old woman, I am in sorrow.
items.analysis: Identical to the reference; keeps the archaic/high-register melancholy and the comic mismatch with the modern setting.

items.translation: They've walled me in... Walled me in, the demons!
items.reference: They've walled me in... Walled me in, the demons!
items.analysis: Identical to the reference; preserves the subject role of “demons” and the panicked, quasi-religious framing of a mundane technical mishap.

items.translation: Behold the power of the life-giving cross!
items.reference: Behold the power of the life-giving cross!
items.analysis: Identical to the reference; keeps the mock-biblical flourish and the joke of attributing a mechanical event to religious miracle.

items.translation: The floor is open to everyone!
items.reference: The floor is open to everyone!
items.analysis: Identical to the reference; successfully conveys the emcee-like imperative and the mass-participation vibe rather than a literal “Everyone dances,” which would be flatter.

items.translation: Now, this was a good call!
items.reference: Now, this was a good call!
items.analysis: Identical to the reference; keeps the breezy, self-satisfied criminal opportunism and the idiomatic feel.

items.original: Икра чёрная, красная… Да! Заморская икра… баклажанная!
items.translation: Black caviar, red caviar... and yes! Overseas spread... eggplant!
items.reference: Black caviar, red caviar... and yes! Overseas spread... eggplant!
items.analysis: Identical to the reference; crucially avoids calling the eggplant spread “caviar,” preserving the intended Soviet-status inversion without confusing non-Russian viewers.

items.original: Три магнитофона, три кинокамеры, три портсигара отечественных, куртка… замшевая… Три
items.translation: Three tape recorders, three movie cameras, three cigarette cases, domestic... a suede jacket... Three.
items.reference: Three tape recorders, three movie cameras, three cigarette cases, domestic... a suede jacket... Three.
items.analysis: Identical to the reference; preserves the comic inventory rhythm, the self-important “domestic” qualifier, and the trailing “Three” that signals exaggeration/instability in the count.

items.original: И тебя вылечат… и тебя тоже вылечат… и меня вылечат…
items.translation: And they’ll cure you... and they’ll cure you too... and they’ll cure me...
items.reference: And they’ll cure you... and they’ll cure you too... and they’ll cure me...
items.analysis: Identical to the reference; keeps the calm, sing-song resignation that turns a dark institutional threat into shared absurdity.

summary.interpretation: 0.18 MQM penalty points per meaning unit (≈ 0.04 major errors per unit; weights: minor=1, major=5, critical=10)
